2606.27287.title: Prompt Injection in Automated Résumé Screening with Large Language Models: Single and Multi-Injection Settings
2606.27287.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used to screen and rank job applicants, creating incentives for candidates to strategically manipulate algorithmic hiring systems. We study prompt injection in automated résumé screening, defined as subtle self-promotional text that introduces no new qualifications but is designed to influence LLM evaluations. Using controlled experiments, we show that prompt injection reliably improves applicant rankings when résumé quality is homogeneous and few candidates inject. However, its effectiveness rapidly diminishes as more candidates inject, collapsing when manipulation becomes widespread. When candidate quality is heterogeneous, prompt injection is less effective on average, but can occasionally allow lower-quality candidates to outrank higher-quality ones, raising fairness concerns. Overall, LLM-based screening is most vulnerable when manipulation is rare and candidate quality differences are small. Code and resources are publicly available at: https://github.com/preetb1199/Prompt_Injection_ACL26
2606.27287.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）越來越多地被用來篩選和排名求職者，這使得候選人有動機策略性地操縱算法招聘系統。我們研究自動化簡歷篩選中的提示注入，這被定義為微妙的自我推銷文本，並不引入新的資格，但旨在影響LLM評估。通過控制實驗，我們顯示當簡歷質量同質且少數候選人進行注入時，提示注入可靠地提高了申請者的排名。然而，隨著更多候選人進行注入，其有效性迅速減少，當操縱變得普遍時則崩潰。當候選人質量異質時，提示注入的平均效果較差，但偶爾可以讓低質量候選人超越高質量候選人，這引發了公平性問題。總的來說，基於LLM的篩選在操縱稀少且候選人質量差異小的情況下最為脆弱。代碼和資源可在以下網址公開獲得：https://github.com/preetb1199/Prompt_Injection_ACL26
2606.27287.author: Preet Baxi et.al.
2606.27287.authors: Preet Baxi, Jiannan Xu, Jane Yi Jiang, Stefanus Jasin
